content: Define the Verilog module TopModule with the specified interface including clk, reset, and q[3:0] as output register.
source: Implement a module named TopModule with the following interface: - input clk - input reset - output reg [3:0] q

content: Implement a 4-bit register to hold the current state of the decade counter. This register should be updated on the positive edge of clk and reset to 0 when reset is active-high.
source: The module implements a decade counter that cycles from 0 to 9. All state transitions occur on the positive edge of clk. The reset signal is active-high and synchronous.

content: Implement the combinational logic to determine the next state of the counter. This logic should increment the counter by 1 when the current state is less than 9, and wrap around to 0 when the current state reaches 9.
source: The module implements a decade counter that cycles from 0 to 9.

content: Connect the output of the combinational logic to the input of the 4-bit register, ensuring that the logic depth is minimized to allow higher clock frequencies.
source: Optimize the design for performance: - Minimize the combinational logic depth in the clock-to-q path.
